title: libpod/container_internal: Split locale at the first dot, etc.
body:
We're going to feed this into Go's BCP 47 language parser.  [Language tags have the form][1]:

```abnf
langtag = language
          ["-" script]
          ["-" region]
          *("-" variant)
          *("-" extension)
          ["-" privateuse]
```

and [locales have the form][2]:

    [language[_territory][.codeset][@modifier]]

The modifier is useful for collation, but Go's language-based API (golang/go#25340) does not provide a way for us to supply it.  This code converts our locale to a BCP 47 language by stripping the dot and later and replacing the first underscore, if any, with a hyphen.  This will avoid errors like #2494:

    WARN[0000] failed to parse language "en_US.UTF-8": language: tag is not well-formed

when feeding `language.Parse(...)`.

CC @mtrmac.  While #2501 is hashing out whether or not to keep locale-based collation, I thought I might as well fix the bug in the current implementation ;).

Fixes #2494.

[1]: https://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47#section-2.1
[2]: http://pubs.opengroup.org/onlinepubs/9699919799/basedefs/V1_chap08.html#tag_08_02

Alright, what happened here? Did the bot ignore the yellow tests?

> Alright, what happened here? Did the bot ignore the yellow tests?

What yellow tests?  And Tide might only respect Prow tests, I'm not sure.
I'd just restarted a failing test that usually takes around 40 minutes to pass... I think it didn't register that and merged anyways.
